s2.ptxt: Install the front lower ball joint to the steering knuckle with the nut.

atten3.ptxt: Prevent oil from adhering to the screw and tapered parts.

s2.ptxt: Install a new cotter pin.

atten3.ptxt: If the holes for the cotter pin are not aligned, tighten the nut further up to 60°.

s2.ptxt: Using SST and a press, install a new front axle hub bearing to the steering knuckle.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL FRONT DISC BRAKE DUST COVER

s2.ptxt: Install the disc brake dust cover to the steering knuckle with the 4 bolts.
Using SST and a press, install the front axle hub sub-assembly.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL FRONT AXLE HUB HOLE SNAP RING

s2.ptxt: Using snap ring pliers, install a new front axle hub hole snap ring.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL FRONT WHEEL NO. 1 BEARING DUST DEFLECTOR

s2.ptxt: Using SST and a hammer, install a new No. 1 front wheel bearing dust deflector.

atten4.ptxt: Align the hole for the speed sensor in the No. 1 front wheel bearing dust deflector with the steering knuckle.

s2.ptxt: Align the matchmarks and install the front drive shaft assembly to the front axle hub sub-assembly.

atten3.ptxt: Be careful not to damage the drive shaft boot and speed sensor rotor.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL FRONT SUSPENSION LOWER NO. 1 ARM

s2.ptxt: Install the lower ball joint to the front suspension lower No. 1 arm with the bolt and 2 nuts.
Install the tie rod end sub-assembly to the steering knuckle with the nut.
Install a new cotter pin.

atten3.ptxt: If the holes for the cotter pin are not aligned, tighten the nut up to 60° further.

s2.ptxt: Align the matchmarks and install the front disc.

atten3.ptxt: When replacing the disc with a new one, select the installation position where the front disc has the minimum runout.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL FRONT DISC BRAKE CALIPER ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Install the front disc brake caliper assembly to the steering knuckle with the 2 bolts.

atten3.ptxt: Do not twist the brake hose when installing the front disc brake caliper assembly.

s2.ptxt: Install the front speed sensor to the steering knuckle with the bolt.

item.ptxt: Prevent foreign matter from adhering to the speed sensor.
Be careful not to damage the speed sensor.

s2.ptxt: Install the flexible hose and the speed sensor to the shock absorber with the bolt and set the sensor clip on the knuckle.

item.ptxt: Be careful not to damage the speed sensor.
Prevent foreign matter from adhering to the speed sensor.
Do not twist the sensor wire when installing the speed sensor.

s2.ptxt: Using a socket wrench (30 mm), install a new axle hub nut.

atten4.ptxt: Stake the nut after inspecting for looseness and runout in the following steps.

s-1.ptxt: SEPARATE FRONT DISC BRAKE CALIPER ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Remove the 2 bolts and separate the front disc brake caliper assembly from the steering knuckle.

atten3.ptxt: Use wire or an equivalent tool to keep the brake caliper from hanging down by the flexible hose.

s2.ptxt: Remove the front disc.

atten4.ptxt: Put matchmarks on the disc and the axle hub.

s-1.ptxt: INSPECT FRONT AXLE HUB BEARING LOOSENESS

s2.ptxt: Using a dial indicator, check for looseness near the center of the axle hub.

atten3.ptxt: Ensure that the dial indicator is set perpendicular to the measurement surface.

atten4.ptxt: If looseness exceeds the maximum, replace the bearing.

s2.ptxt: Using a dial indicator, check for runout on the surface of the axle hub outside the hub bolt.

atten3.ptxt: Ensure that the dial indicator is set perpendicular to the measurement surface.

atten4.ptxt: If runout exceeds the maximum, replace the axle hub.

s2.ptxt: Align the matchmarks and install the front disc.

atten3.ptxt: When replacing the disc with a new one, select the installation position where the front disc has the minimum runout.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL FRONT DISC BRAKE CALIPER ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Install the front disc brake caliper assembly to the steering knuckle with the 2 bolts.

atten3.ptxt: Do not twist the brake hose when installing the front disc brake caliper assembly.

s2.ptxt: Using a chisel and hammer, stake the axle hub nut.

s-1.ptxt: INSPECT AND ADJUST FRONT WHEEL ALIGNMENT
